summary_en: Lake Merced's archive record is dominated by water and environmental studies. A 1999 watershed sanitary survey maps potential pollution sources; a 1998 baseline inventory catalogs the lake's soils, vegetation, wildlife and fisheries; a 1993 water-resource planning study and a 1990s groundwater master plan track its surface elevation and water levels; and a 1998 memorandum weighs alternatives to raise the lake, costed at $2,200,000, $8,200,000 and $3,900,000, alongside a 2001 Vista Grande diversion study. A 1973 waste-water master plan sites a large treatment plant at Lake Merced, and a 1965 annual report logs fishing-permit revenue and float repairs. Operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line totals about $250M.

documents.title: San Francisco master plan for waste water management
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Dept. of Public Works
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1973 waste-water master plan sites the city's single large treatment plant at the Lake Merced area (south of the zoo), sized to treat one billion gallons per day in wet weather.

documents.title: San Francisco groundwater master plan : draft / prepared by San Francisco Water Department
documents.creator: San Francisco Water Department
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A groundwater master plan tracks changes in Lake Merced's surface elevation and calls for field investigation and modeling to help restore the lake's water levels.

documents.title: Annual report
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Dept
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1965 Recreation and Park annual report logs Lake Merced's aquatic attendance (122,658) and fishing-permit revenue ($6,302) and repairs to floats at the lake.

documents.title: Lake Merced watershed sanitary survey
documents.creator: Camp, Dresser & McKee,San Francisco Public Utilities Commission
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1999 watershed sanitary survey identifies potential pollution sources around Lake Merced to protect its water-supply, recreation and wildlife uses.

documents.title: Lake Merced 1998 baseline natural resources inventory
documents.creator: prepared by: Trihey & Associates, Inc., through: Camp Dresser & McKee,prepared for: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1998 baseline natural-resources inventory maps Lake Merced's soils, vegetation, wildlife and fisheries.

documents.title: Lake Merced water resource planning study
documents.creator: San Francisco Water Department,prepared by GEO/Resource Consultants, Inc. in association with Montgomery/Watson, Jones and Stokes Associates, Inc., Public Affairs Management
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1993 water-resource planning study evaluates lake-use and water-quality issues and the hydrology of Lake Merced for the Water Department.

documents.title: Feasibility evaluation of alternatives to raise Lake Merced : draft
documents.creator: CH2M HILL
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1998 technical memorandum evaluates alternatives to raise and maintain Lake Merced's level, costing options at $2,200,000, $8,200,000 and $3,900,000.

documents.title: Vista Grande diversion feasibility evaluation / prepared for City and County of San Francisco, City of Daly City, County of San Mateo ; prepared by CH2M Hill and the Duffey Company
documents.creator: CH2M HILL (Firm)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 2001 feasibility evaluation studies diverting Vista Grande storm water into Lake Merced and its water-quality effects.

documents.title: Capital plan ... annual update
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Recreation and Park Dept. Capital Improvement Division
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A Recreation and Park capital plan carries a Lake Merced master plan and overlook-and-trail project and situates the Harding Park clubhouse within Lake Merced Park.
